title: neonauclea megaphylla
intro: Neonauclea megaphylla, also known as the "Tawa" tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It is a large shrub or small tree native to the Philippines, which has been used in traditional medicine for centuries due to its medicinal properties. Neonauclea megaphylla is said to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to treat various health conditions such as fever, skin infections, and respiratory issues, providing health benefits like reducing inflammation and fighting off infections. In herbal preparations, the leaves and bark of the Neonauclea megaphylla tree are often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are believed to aid in the treatment of various ailments.
